purpose: Classify, verify, and harden repository mutation governance for the main branch after #695 adversarial verification, #383 reverse-closure map, and #896 Cloudflare containment. Enumerates all repository mutation paths by which main could be altered, classifies each by enforcement status, identifies open sovereignty gaps, and declares required invariants. Evidence only — no runtime, validator, proof, or execution semantics altered.

classification_schema.ENFORCED: Control is technically active at the GitHub platform level and cannot be suppressed by the PR author or repository contributor without root authority. Example: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on pull_request events are enforced by the platform to execute when a qualifying PR is opened.
classification_schema.EXTERNAL_POLICY: Control is defined in governance policy and depends on a human administrator applying it at the GitHub repository or organization settings level. The control is documented and declared but not currently active at the platform level.
classification_schema.BREAK_GLASS: Mutation path exists as a root authority capability that cannot be eliminated by repository-level governance. It is classified as non-normal execution, cannot create MindShift execution legitimacy, and must be recorded as an explicit break-glass event.
classification_schema.OPEN: Mutation path is currently uncontrolled at the platform level. No active enforcement mechanism exists. Represents a live sovereignty gap that must be resolved for governance closure.
classification_schema.CONTAINED: Mutation path has governance controls in place that limit the exploitability surface, but those controls are not fully enforced as blocking gates at the platform level. Evidence is generated but not all merge/push paths are prevented.

governance_invariants.statement: repository mutation alone cannot create legitimacy
governance_invariants.description: A commit, merge, or direct push to main does not produce a MindShift execution authority, proof object, or canonical chain traversal. Repository state change is a precondition for deployment consideration, not a legitimacy grant.
governance_invariants.basis: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json core_invariant: No PREO_VALID -> no merge legitimacy; runtime/root_authority_constraints.json root_authority_escalation=NULL; RCM-001 creates_legitimacy=false, RCM-003 creates_legitimacy=false

governance_invariants.statement: merge capability does not equal execution legitimacy
governance_invariants.description: The ability to merge a PR into main (whether through branch protection, admin bypass, or direct push) grants no authority to execute, deploy, or mutate production state through the MindShift canonical chain. Merge creates repository state; legitimacy requires /session -> /continuity -> /authority -> /compile -> /validate -> /execute -> /proof traversal.
governance_invariants.basis: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json merge_legitimacy_requirements: PREO_VALID generated for exact head_sha; RCM-002 creates_legitimacy=true only because PREO_VALID is the merge gate, not the merge action itself

governance_invariants.statement: branch protection is governance dependency, not proof
governance_invariants.description: Branch protection rules enforce the process by which repository mutations are reviewed and checked. They are a necessary dependency for repository sovereignty but do not themselves constitute proof of execution legitimacy. A branch protection setting cannot substitute for a PREO_VALID, SCO_CANDIDATE, or canonical chain proof object.
governance_invariants.basis: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json status=policy_only_non_enforcing; PREO_VALID remains canonical merge gate independent of branch protection activation; branch protection is governance infrastructure dependency per REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json INV-005

governance_invariants.statement: unsigned and unreviewed mutation paths must classify as unauthorized
governance_invariants.description: Any repository mutation path that lacks cryptographic commit provenance (signed commit) or required CODEOWNERS review must be classified as UNAUTHORIZED. This classification does not block such mutations from occurring (absent branch protection enforcement) but establishes their governance status for evidence and reconciliation purposes.
governance_invariants.basis: BSM-005 unsigned_commits OPEN unauthorized_result=UNVERIFIABLE_COMMIT_PROVENANCE; BSM-004 missing_codeowners_review OPEN unauthorized_result=UNREVIEWED_MERGE; BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json requires_approvals=true, dismiss_stale_reviews=true

governance_invariants.statement: external GitHub admin and root authority must classify as BREAK_GLASS
governance_invariants.description: GitHub repository administrator authority, GitHub organization owner authority, and any account with bypass capability over branch protection rules are root-level authorities external to the MindShift governance model. They must be classified as BREAK_GLASS, acknowledged as non-normal execution paths, and explicitly documented as not creating MindShift execution legitimacy.
governance_invariants.basis: BSM-003 admin_bypass BREAK_GLASS non_normal_execution=true creates_legitimacy=false; RCM-019 root_credential_break_glass BREAK_GLASS; governance/ROOT_AUTHORITY_CLASSIFICATION.json GitHub admin authority bypass_risk=P3; root_authority_constraints.json root_authority_escalation=NULL

mutation_paths.description: Direct git push to the main branch without opening a pull request, bypassing PREO/SCO generation, CODEOWNERS review, and required status checks.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json restrict_direct_push_to_main=true; status=policy_only_non_enforcing; GitHub repository settings not aligned; no platform-level enforcement active.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None enforced at platform level. Policy document only.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Repository owner or collaborator with push access can push directly to main, bypassing PREO/SCO governance checks, CODEOWNERS review, constitutional-integrity classification, and all required status checks. No merge gate is active.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enable GitHub branch protection for main: restrict direct pushes, require pull requests before merge, set required_approving_review_count >= 1 with dismiss_stale_reviews=true, require status checks merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate.

mutation_paths.description: Force push (git push --force or git push --force-with-lease) to main, rewriting git history and potentially erasing governance evidence from the commit graph.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json allow_force_pushes=false; status=policy_only_non_enforcing; GitHub repository settings not aligned; no platform-level enforcement active.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None enforced at platform level. Policy document only.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Force push to main can rewrite git history, erasing PREO/SCO evidence, merge commit records, and proof lineage references from the repository graph. Branch protection advisory only.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce allow_force_pushes=false in GitHub branch protection settings for main.

mutation_paths.description: GitHub repository administrator or organization owner merging or pushing directly to main by overriding branch protection rules, using the 'bypass branch protections' capability, or modifying repository settings to temporarily disable protections.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json allow_admin_bypass=false; rationale: admin bypass creates alternate mutation paths outside governance visibility; this policy is advisory and not yet enforced at GitHub settings level.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Cannot be eliminated against GitHub platform root authority. GitHub repository admin has irrevocable platform-level bypass capability. Classified as non-normal execution path requiring explicit audit log entry.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: GitHub repository administrator has platform-level authority that cannot be constrained by repository-level governance artifacts. Even with branch protection enforced, admin bypass remains a root authority capability. This gap cannot be closed at the repository level; it is classified and acknowledged as BREAK_GLASS.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce allow_admin_bypass=false in GitHub branch protection settings. Document all admin bypass events as explicit break-glass incidents with audit log evidence. Periodic audit of GitHub audit log for bypass events.

mutation_paths.description: Merge of a PR to main without a review and approval from the designated CODEOWNERS maintainer (@joselunasrt8-creator), allowing unreviewed mutations to reach main.
mutation_paths.current_gate: .github/CODEOWNERS and CODEOWNERS define @joselunasrt8-creator as required reviewer for all repository paths and critical governance paths; enforcement depends on branch protection requiring CODEOWNERS approval (required_approving_review_count >= 1 with dismiss_stale_reviews=true).
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: CODEOWNERS review is advisory until branch protection enforces required_approving_review_count >= 1 with dismiss_stale_reviews=true at the GitHub settings level.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Without branch protection enforcement, PRs can be merged without CODEOWNERS approval. The CODEOWNERS file declares the ownership boundary but does not gate the merge action until branch protection is enabled.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection with required_approving_review_count >= 1, dismiss_stale_reviews=true, and CODEOWNERS enforcement. Enables CODEOWNERS from advisory to enforced status.

mutation_paths.description: Commits pushed to main or merged via PR without GPG or SSH cryptographic signatures, making commit authorship and provenance unverifiable.
mutation_paths.current_gate: No signed commit requirement defined in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json or enforced at GitHub settings level. No require_signed_commits policy exists. Unsigned commits are accepted by default.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. No commit signing requirement is defined or enforced.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Commits without cryptographic signatures cannot have their authorship verified. Bot, automated, or impersonated commits cannot be distinguished from legitimate maintainer commits. Commit provenance chain is unverifiable without signing.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Define require_signed_commits policy. Enable GitHub branch protection require_signed_commits setting for main. Require GPG or SSH signing for all commits to main.

mutation_paths.description: A PR review approval granted before a subsequent push to the PR branch remaining valid and allowing merge with stale (pre-mutation) review evidence.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json dismiss_stale_reviews=true; prevents stale reviews from pre-push state from satisfying required review count after new commits are pushed; requires branch protection enforcement to activate.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Defined in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json. Depends on GitHub branch protection settings being aligned at the repository level. Currently advisory.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Without branch protection enforcement, dismiss_stale_reviews=true has no effect. A reviewer's approval of a pre-mutation commit state persists and could be used to satisfy review requirements for a different (post-mutation) head SHA.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection with dismiss_stale_reviews=true. Activate this control by applying BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json in GitHub repository settings.

mutation_paths.description: Required GitHub Actions status checks (merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate) that must pass before a PR can be merged, ensuring PREO/SCO evidence is generated and governance checks pass.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json required_status_checks: [merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate]; emitted_check_inventory documents workflow file / job name / check run name alignment; GitHub settings not aligned; status checks run on PRs but are not required merge gates.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Defined in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json. Depends on GitHub repository branch protection settings being configured with the exact required check run names matching emitted job names. Currently advisory.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Status checks run on every PR to main and generate PREO/SCO evidence, but they are not required for merge. An admin or owner can merge a PR even if merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, or generate-sco-candidate fail or have not completed.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection with required_status_checks: [merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate] and require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge=true. Verify check run name alignment with emitted_check_inventory.

mutation_paths.description: Modification of GitHub Actions workflow files (.github/workflows/**) through a PR to main, potentially introducing privilege escalation, ungoverned deploy paths, or removal of governance checks.
mutation_paths.current_gate: constitutional-integrity.yml classifies .github/workflows/** changes as source_control_governance mutations (PR-3 sovereignty class); preo-candidate.yml generates PREO_CANDIDATE for every PR to main; sco-candidate.yml generates SCO_CANDIDATE with workflow_mutation class when .github/workflows/** changes detected; CODEOWNERS requires @joselunasrt8-creator approval for /.github/workflows/**.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: constitutional-integrity.yml and preo/sco workflows run on every PR and classify workflow mutations. Evidence is generated. Merge is not gated by classification result until branch protection required_status_checks are enforced.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Workflow mutation detection is operational but merge is not blocked pending branch protection enforcement. A workflow change classified as PR-3 sovereignty generates PREO/SCO evidence but does not prevent merge without required checks enforcement.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection required_status_checks including constitutional-integrity check. Require CODEOWNERS approval for .github/workflows/** via branch protection. Validate that governed-deploy.yml workflow mutations are subject to PR-3 classification review.

mutation_paths.description: Deletion of the main branch or other governance-relevant branches, destroying the git history and PREO/SCO evidence associated with those branches.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json allow_branch_deletion=false; status=policy_only_non_enforcing; no platform-level enforcement active.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None enforced at platform level. Policy document only.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Main branch or feature branches containing governance evidence (PREO candidates, SCO candidates, proof lineage references) could be deleted, destroying history. No platform-level protection is active against branch deletion.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce allow_branch_deletion=false in GitHub branch protection settings for main.

mutation_paths.description: Deletion and recreation of a branch (particularly main) with a different commit history, substituting the lineage with an alternative history that bypasses governance evidence.
mutation_paths.current_gate: Same as BSM-009 branch_deletion. No protection against recreation of governance-relevant branches with divergent histories. No append-only constraint on repository history at the platform level.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Depends on branch_deletion prevention (BSM-009). If deletion is prevented, recreation with different history is also prevented.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: A branch could be deleted and recreated with a different commit history, replacing all governance evidence with an alternative lineage. No append-only constraint at the repository level prevents this.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce allow_branch_deletion=false via branch protection. This also prevents recreation with different history. Periodic git history integrity verification.

mutation_paths.description: Creation of git tags or GitHub releases without canonical chain traversal, PREO validation, or proof lineage, producing release artifacts with unverifiable provenance.
mutation_paths.current_gate: No governed tag or release creation workflow exists. GitHub allows any repository writer to create tags. No signed-tag requirement. No release provenance attestation boundary defined. No PREO/SCO gate on tag creation.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Tag creation is ungoverned. Linked to #382 for release provenance governance definition.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Tags and GitHub releases can be created without canonical chain traversal, PREO validation, or proof lineage. Release artifacts cannot be traced back to governed repository state. No signed-tag or DSSE attestation requirement exists.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Define release provenance and artifact attestation boundary per #382. Require signed tags or DSSE attestations. Require passing validation evidence and proof lineage reference before release creation.

mutation_paths.description: Exploitation of GitHub Actions workflow permissions to escalate privileges, access secrets, or execute production mutations outside the canonical chain — including through write permissions on the GITHUB_TOKEN, secrets access, or OIDC token escalation.
mutation_paths.current_gate: governed-deploy.yml: requires workflow_dispatch event, DECISION_ID, VALIDATED_OBJECT_HASH, INVOCATION_NONCE; full canonical chain (/session -> /proof) with replay protection; MINDSHIFT_GOVERNED_DEPLOY_CONTEXT=github_actions_governed required by governed-deploy.ts; permission scopes limit GITHUB_TOKEN; constitutional-integrity.yml detects workflow permission changes.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Workflow code enforces canonical chain traversal. Context variable isolates governed execution. governed-deploy.ts blocks non-governed context. Privilege escalation through workflow is detected by constitutional-integrity.yml classification.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Workflow definition mutation through PR (BSM-008) could introduce privilege escalation in governed-deploy.yml or prepare-governed-deploy.yml. Requires branch protection enforcement (BSM-007) to gate workflow changes through CODEOWNERS review and required checks.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection required checks and CODEOWNERS review for .github/workflows/** (BSM-007, BSM-008). Maintain GITHUB_TOKEN minimum permission scopes. Add GitHub environment approval gate to governed-deploy.yml.

mutation_paths.description: Triggering governed-deploy.yml or prepare-governed-deploy.yml with fabricated inputs (decision_id, validated_object_hash, invocation_nonce) or dispatching ungoverned workflows to achieve production mutations outside the canonical chain.
mutation_paths.current_gate: governed-deploy.yml verifies event_name=workflow_dispatch, CALLER_WORKFLOW_REF, and full canonical chain input validation; fabricated inputs fail /compile hash match requirement; replay protection via used nonces and deployment_proof_registry UNIQUE proof_binding_hash; prepare-governed-deploy.yml does not deploy autonomously.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Canonical chain verification within governed-deploy.yml; /compile hash match blocks fabricated object hashes; nonce-based replay protection; governed-deploy.ts context guard prevents local terminal execution.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Repeated prepare-governed-deploy.yml dispatches generate multiple valid deploy-candidate tuples. Each tuple is reusable until expiry or first consumption. No one-shot gate on tuple generation. Approval gate before deploy step is absent.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Rate-limit or one-shot-gate prepare-governed-deploy.yml dispatch. Add GitHub environment approval gate to governed-deploy.yml production environment. Expire generated tuples after first consume.

mutation_paths.description: Merging PRs to main without passing through a configured merge queue that would enforce head-SHA-bound required checks against the current enqueued state, allowing merge with stale check evidence.
mutation_paths.current_gate: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json require_merge_queue_required_checks_on_enqueued_head=true; squash_merge_requires_current_head_sha_checks=true; rebase_merge_requires_current_head_sha_checks=true; branch protection not enforced; no merge queue configured at GitHub settings level.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Merge queue policy is defined in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json but not configured at GitHub settings. Depends on branch protection activation.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Without merge queue configuration, PRs can be merged using check results from a different head SHA than the one being merged. Squash and rebase merges can complete with stale review or check evidence.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Enforce branch protection and configure merge queue per BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json merge_method_policy. Enable squash_merge_requires_current_head_sha_checks and rebase_merge_requires_current_head_sha_checks.

mutation_paths.description: Automated bot accounts or machine accounts with repository write access pushing commits or merging PRs without human review, bypassing the CODEOWNERS maintainer review requirement.
mutation_paths.current_gate: CODEOWNERS requires @joselunasrt8-creator review for all repository paths; GitHub collaborator settings control which accounts have push access; no machine account restriction or bot-specific governance policy defined in repository artifacts.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Depends on GitHub repository collaborator settings and organization policy. No bot-specific constraint is defined within repository governance artifacts. CODEOWNERS review enforcement depends on branch protection (BSM-004).
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Bots or automated accounts with repository write access could push commits or open and merge PRs without human CODEOWNERS review. Bot mutation classification depends on collaborator access control at the GitHub organization/repository level, external to repository governance code.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: Restrict repository collaborator access to named human accounts only. Enforce CODEOWNERS review via branch protection. Classify all machine account mutations as requiring explicit governance exception.

mutation_paths.description: GitHub Actions workflow execution triggered by pull_request events to main — specifically constitutional-integrity.yml, preo-candidate.yml, sco-candidate.yml, and merge-governance-check.yml — which run on every qualifying PR and generate governance evidence.
mutation_paths.current_gate: GitHub platform enforces workflow trigger semantics: on: pull_request targeting main causes workflows to run on every qualifying PR event. Workflow execution cannot be suppressed by the PR author. Evidence (PREO_CANDIDATE, SCO_CANDIDATE, constitutional drift classification) is generated on every PR.
mutation_paths.enforcement_mechanism: GitHub Actions platform enforces trigger-based workflow execution. The PR author cannot prevent these workflows from running. PR workflow execution is technically enforced by the platform independent of branch protection settings.
mutation_paths.residual_gap: Workflow EXECUTION is enforced by the platform. Workflow RESULTS as required merge gates are not enforced (classified as EXTERNAL_POLICY per BSM-007). Evidence generation is active; merge blocking is inactive until branch protection required_status_checks are configured.
mutation_paths.closure_condition: No closure action required for execution enforcement itself. Closure of the merge-gating gap requires branch protection enforcement per BSM-007.

residual_open_sovereignty_gaps.closure_requires: BREAK_GLASS classification only; cannot be eliminated at repository level
GitHub branch protection enforcement with required_approving_review_count >= 1
GitHub branch protection and merge queue configuration

summary.note: Branch protection activation (GitHub repository settings) closes RSGAP-001, RSGAP-002, RSGAP-004, RSGAP-006, RSGAP-008 as a single governance action. RSGAP-003 (admin_bypass) cannot be closed at the repository level — it is classified as BREAK_GLASS. RSGAP-005 (unsigned commits) requires a separate branch protection setting. RSGAP-007 (tag/release) is tracked under #382.
summary.issue_380_closure_condition: #380 closes when all repository mutation paths are classified in this matrix, residual open sovereignty gaps are explicitly identified, and branch mutation governance is fully mapped and verified.
